request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?pkg ?name (COUNT(DISTINCT ?child) AS ?childCount)
WHERE {
  ?pkg rdf:type vocab:package .
  ?pkg vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  OPTIONAL { ?pkg vocab:Package_packagedElement+ ?child . }
}
GROUP BY ?pkg ?name
ORDER BY DESC(?childCount)
LIMIT 25

request.message: The query returned 25 package-like elements (rdf:type vocab:package) with their names and counts of packaged child elements. This strongly indicates we’re seeing the model’s main/high-level packages, though the true set may be larger than 25 due to the LIMIT.
request.reason: The user asked for the main packages; the results provide concrete examples with their names and sizes, sorted by number of contained elements. We can report these top packages from the query output.

request.message: Finalizing with the top packages returned (LIMIT 25) and reporting representative examples with their child counts. Note: the query is truncated, so these are the largest packages seen in the sample, not necessarily the complete full list.
request.reason: We have concrete package names and their relative sizes (child counts) from the SPARQL results, which directly answers “main packages”. Although truncated to 25 rows, the reported packages are clearly among the most substantial/high-level packages.
